intro: Houstonia mullerae, also known as Muller's bluet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as wounds and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Houstonia mullerae include gentiopicrin, loganic acid, and swertiamarin, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Muller's bluet is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to take advantage of its purported health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
